evals.expected_output: All three tasks in plan.md are checked off. spec.md status is set to 'done'. Source files (tasks-store.js, tasks route, test file) are created. Acceptance criteria in spec.md are checked. A 'Development Notes' section is appended to spec.md. context.md Last updated date is updated.
expectations: All tasks in plan.md are checked (- [x])
All acceptance criteria in spec.md are checked (- [x])
context.md Last updated field is updated (not left as 2026-03-20)
A DELETE route or handler is present somewhere in the output files

evals.expected_output: The skill detects the in-progress state, shows resumption progress (2/4 tasks done), and implements only the two remaining unchecked test tasks. All tasks end up checked. spec.md status becomes 'done'. Dev notes are appended. The already-checked tasks are NOT re-run.
expectations: The skill announces it is resuming (shows progress like '2/4 tasks complete' or similar)
The two already-checked tasks are still checked and were not re-run
All four tasks in plan.md are checked (- [x]) at the end
A Jest test file for the GET /notes/:id route is written

evals.expected_output: The skill reads create-post's spec.md, detects that 'user-registration' in requires is not yet done (status: ready), and stops without implementing anything. It lists the unmet dependency and its current status, and informs the user to implement user-registration first.
expectations: The skill does NOT start implementing create-post (no source files written)
create-post/spec.md status is NOT changed to 'in-progress' or 'done'
The skill mentions 'user-registration' as an unmet dependency
The skill informs the user they need to implement the dependency first
The skill mentions the current status of the unmet dependency (ready or blocked)
